annotations.label: Cardinality of the continuum

anchor.snippet: the cardinality of the continuum is the cardinality or

annotations.note: `Cardinal.continuum` (notation `𝔠`) is defined as `2 ^ ℵ₀`, the cardinality of the continuum.

annotations.label: Continuum equals power set of naturals

anchor.snippet: has the same number of elements as the power set of

annotations.note: `Cardinal.mk_set_nat : #(Set ℕ) = 𝔠` states the power set of ℕ has cardinality continuum.

annotations.label: Equal cardinality via bijection

anchor.snippet: two sets have the same cardinality if, and only if, there exists a bijective function between them

annotations.note: `Cardinal.eq : #α = #β ↔ Nonempty (α ≃ β)` expresses that equal cardinality is equivalent to existence of a bijection.

annotations.label: Open interval equinumerous with R

anchor.snippet: the open interval ( a , b ) is equinumerous with

annotations.note: `Cardinal.mk_Ioo_real {a b : ℝ} (h : a < b) : #(Ioo a b) = 𝔠` gives the open interval the same cardinality as ℝ (= 𝔠).

annotations.label: Continuum hypothesis (no intermediate cardinality)

anchor.snippet: which asserts that there are no sets whose cardinality is strictly between

annotations.note: The continuum hypothesis is not stated in Mathlib (it is independent of its type theory); only `aleph0_lt_continuum` and `aleph_one_le_continuum` bracket 𝔠.

annotations.label: Uncountability of the reals

anchor.snippet: the set of real numbers is uncountably infinite

annotations.note: `Cardinal.not_countable_real` (and the `Uncountable ℝ` instance) prove the reals are uncountable.

annotations.label: Cantor's theorem

anchor.snippet: which states that the cardinality of any set is strictly less than that of its power set

annotations.note: `Cardinal.cantor (a : Cardinal) : a < 2 ^ a` is exactly Cantor's theorem that a set is smaller than its power set.

annotations.label: Cantor–Bernstein–Schroeder theorem

anchor.snippet: two sets with one-to-one mappings in both directions have the same cardinality

annotations.note: `Function.Embedding.schroeder_bernstein` produces a bijection from injections in both directions (with `Function.Embedding.antisymm` giving the equiv).

annotations.label: Cardinal equality via cardinal arithmetic

anchor.snippet: can be demonstrated using cardinal arithmetic

annotations.note: `Cardinal.mk_real : #ℝ = 𝔠` is proved using cardinal arithmetic (with `two_power_aleph0 : 2 ^ ℵ₀ = 𝔠`).

annotations.label: Further cardinal arithmetic identities

anchor.snippet: By using the rules of cardinal arithmetic, one can also show that

annotations.note: Identities like `continuum_power_aleph0 : 𝔠 ^ ℵ₀ = 𝔠`, `continuum_mul_self`, `aleph0_mul_continuum`, and `nat_power_aleph0` are all proven in Continuum.lean.

annotations.label: Every real has an infinite decimal expansion

anchor.snippet: Every real number has at least one infinite decimal expansion

annotations.note: `Real.digits`/`Real.ofDigits_digits` give a base-`b` digit expansion recovering any real in [0,1), but not a general 'every real has an infinite decimal expansion' statement.

annotations.label: Countably many decimal places

anchor.snippet: the number of decimal places is countable since they can be put into a one-to-one correspondence with the set of natural numbers

annotations.note: Digit sequences in Mathlib (`Real.ofDigits`) are indexed by ℕ and ℕ is countable, but this informal step is not itself a Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: Integer part plus decimal fraction

anchor.snippet: Since each real number can be broken into an integer part and a decimal fraction, we get

annotations.note: The cardinal identity used (`aleph0_mul_continuum : ℵ₀ * 𝔠 = 𝔠`) is formalized, but the integer-part/decimal-fraction decomposition argument is not stated.

annotations.label: Lower bound via 3/7 decimal fractions

anchor.snippet: decimal fractions containing only 3 or 7 are only a part of the real numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the analogous lower bound 𝔠 ≤ #ℝ via `cantorFunction_injective`, an injection {0,1}^ℕ → ℝ using base-1/3 expansions (used inside `mk_real`).

annotations.label: Beth numbers sequence

anchor.snippet: The sequence of beth numbers is defined by setting

annotations.note: `Cardinal.beth` is defined with `beth_zero : ℶ_ 0 = ℵ₀`, `beth_succ : ℶ_ (succ o) = 2 ^ ℶ_ o`, and `beth_limit`.

annotations.label: Beth-two as power set of R

anchor.snippet: The third beth number, beth-two , is the cardinality of the power set of

annotations.note: `beth_one : ℶ_ 1 = 𝔠` and `beth_succ` give `ℶ_ 2 = 2 ^ 𝔠`, equal to `#(Set ℝ)` via `mk_powerset`, but no single decl states beth-two = cardinality of 𝒫(ℝ).

annotations.label: Continuum hypothesis (second aleph)

anchor.snippet: the continuum hypothesis states that there is no set

annotations.note: CH (𝔠 = ℵ₁) is not formalized; Mathlib only has the inequality `aleph_one_le_continuum : ℵ₁ ≤ 𝔠`.

annotations.label: Independence of CH from ZFC

anchor.snippet: This statement is now known to be independent of the axioms of Zermelo–Fraenkel set theory with the axiom of choice

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize forcing/independence results, so the independence of CH from ZFC is absent.

annotations.label: König's theorem on cofinality

anchor.snippet: equality can be ruled out by König's theorem on the grounds of cofinality

annotations.note: König's theorem `Cardinal.sum_lt_prod` is formalized, and its cofinality consequence `Cardinal.lt_cof_ord_power` gives ℵ₀ < cf(𝔠).

annotations.label: Sets with cardinality of the continuum

anchor.section: Sets with cardinality of the continuum
anchor.snippet: A great many sets studied in mathematics have cardinality equal to

annotations.note: Many such sets are formalized (`mk_real`, `mk_complex`, the interval lemmas `mk_Ioo_real`, etc.), all equal to 𝔠.

annotations.label: Real algebraic numbers are countable

anchor.section: Sets with cardinality of the continuum
anchor.snippet: The set of real algebraic numbers is countably infinite

annotations.note: `Algebraic.cardinalMk_of_countable_of_charZero : #{x : A // IsAlgebraic R x} = ℵ₀` (with `Algebraic.countable`) gives the real algebraic numbers (R=ℚ, A=ℝ) as countably infinite.

annotations.label: Real transcendental numbers have cardinality c

anchor.section: Sets with cardinality of the continuum
anchor.snippet: the real algebraic numbers and the real transcendental numbers are disjoint sets whose union is

annotations.note: Algebraic numbers are countable and ℝ is uncountable, so transcendentals have cardinality 𝔠, but this conclusion is not stated as a Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Cardinality of Euclidean space

anchor.section: Sets with cardinality of the continuum
anchor.snippet: Per Cantor's proof of the cardinality of Euclidean space

annotations.note: `mk_complex : #ℂ = 𝔠` formalizes the n=2 case (#ℝ² = 𝔠); a general `#(Fin n → ℝ) = 𝔠` for Euclidean space is not stated.

annotations.label: Sets with greater cardinality

anchor.snippet: Sets with cardinality greater than

annotations.note: `Cardinal.cantor` gives 𝔠 < 2 ^ 𝔠, establishing that sets of cardinality greater than 𝔠 (e.g. 𝒫(ℝ)) exist.

annotations.label: These sets have cardinality beth-two

anchor.snippet: These all have cardinality

annotations.note: `mk_powerset : #(𝒫 s) = 2 ^ #s` yields #(𝒫 ℝ) = 2 ^ 𝔠 (= beth-two), but the identification with beth-two and the full collection of such sets is not stated.
